title: Creamy Mexican Ground Beef Tacos
description: A nutty and velvety weeknight Mexican dish featuring ground annatto and a flexitarian-friendly twist, shaped like nigiri for a unique presentation.
instructions: Brown the ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the annatto powder, onion, and garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the taco seasoning and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Reduce heat to low and stir in the sour cream, letting it simmer for 5-7 minutes, until the mixture is creamy and heated through.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the beef mixture onto a tortilla and topping with shredded cheese.
Use your hands to shape each taco into a nigiri-like shape, applying gentle pressure to form a compact shape.
Garnish with additional sour cream, cheese, and any other desired toppings.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
